March 2020 hits and suddenly everyone's working from home. Except wait, plot twist: there ARE no jobs to work from home with. Unemployment rockets to 14.8% faster than you can say 'flatten the curve.'

Companies are scrambling but their supply chains just evaporated into thin air. Three quarters of businesses are like 'cool, all our stuff is stuck in a shipping container somewhere in the Pacific.' The global economy is basically playing Jenga blindfolded.
Meanwhile oil prices are having their own little breakdown, crashing 85% because nobody's driving anywhere. Traders are literally paying people to take oil off their hands like it's expired milk.

Restaurants are getting absolutely demolished. We're talking $225 billion in losses because apparently 'contactless delivery' doesn't work when nobody has money. The entire hospitality industry just got hospitalized.
So naturally everyone panic-buys groceries like the apocalypse is coming. Shelves are empty, prices shoot up 4.3%, and suddenly your weekly shopping trip costs more than your car payment used to.

And after all this economic carnage, supply chain chaos, and global market meltdown, you know what really gets me? My turkey sandwich at the deli now costs 15 cents more. FIFTEEN CENTS. I survived a pandemic for THIS?
